name: Problem Amplification
summary: A questioning technique that helps the buyer feel the business consequences of the current problem more clearly so urgency becomes real.
whenToUseIt: Use problem amplification after the buyer has admitted a pain point but before the value case or close plan is fully built. It is especially useful when the status quo still feels safer than change.
steps: Confirm the current problem in the buyer's own language.
Explore the downstream impact on time, revenue, risk, team morale, or customer outcomes.
Ask what happens if the problem stays unresolved for another quarter or year.
example: A buyer admits forecast misses are frustrating. You amplify by asking how those misses affect hiring decisions, board confidence, and manager credibility if they continue through the next two quarters.
